steps.title: Recognize complementary angles from cos(Q) = sin(R)
steps.content:
The equation $\cos(Q) = \sin(R)$ indicates that Q and R are complementary angles. Using the identity $\sin(\theta) = \cos(90° - \theta)$:
$$Q + R = 90°$$

steps.title: Substitute and solve for x
steps.content:
Use the angle measures $Q = x + 61$ and $R = 4x + 4$ in the complementary angle equation:
$$(x + 61) + (4x + 4) = 90$$
Simplify:
$$5x + 65 = 90$$
$$5x = 25$$
$$x = 5$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
